name: youtube_scraper
title: YouTube Scraper
description: Search public YouTube results or inspect a public channel videos tab and return normalized video metadata: title, URL, channel, duration, views, published time, thumbnail, and description snippets.

seo.title: YouTube Scraper Tool for Public Video Metadata
seo.description: Scrape public YouTube search results or channel videos with Better Fetch and return normalized titles, URLs, channel names, durations, views, published times, thumbnails, and snippets.
seo.intro: YouTube Scraper gives agents a focused way to collect public YouTube video metadata without using a private account. It renders search results or a channel videos tab through Better Fetch, parses YouTube's public page data, deduplicates video cards, and returns normalized fields that are easy to save, compare, or pass into an LLM workflow.

useCases.title: Video research
useCases.description: Search YouTube for a topic and return structured video cards for market research, content planning, or source discovery.

useCases.title: Channel monitoring
useCases.description: Inspect a public channel videos tab and capture recent titles, URLs, view counts, durations, thumbnails, and publish-time labels.

useCases.title: Agent briefs
useCases.description: Let an agent gather public YouTube results and summarize visible metadata before deciding which videos need deeper review.

faqs.question: Does YouTube Scraper download video files?
faqs.answer: No. It returns public metadata from YouTube pages and does not download video media, captions, private analytics, or account-only data.

faqs.question: Can it scrape private or age-gated videos?
faqs.answer: No. The tool is designed for public YouTube pages visible to the Better Fetch browser session without user-specific authentication.

faqs.question: Why are view counts returned as text and numbers?
faqs.answer: YouTube often renders compact labels such as 1.2M views. The tool preserves the visible text and also returns a best-effort integer when it can parse one.

query.description: YouTube search query. Use either query or channel_url.

channel_url.description: Public YouTube channel URL. The tool reads its videos tab.

source.description: Search query or channel URL used
